cards.title: Red Light Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Wavelength: 620–750 nm.
Action: Stimulates sympathetic nervous system, raises body temperature, increases RBC production, boosts circulation.
Indications: Anaemia, paralysis, depression, hypothyroidism, chronic fatigue, poor circulation, respiratory disorders.
Duration: 15–30 min. Maintain 60–90 cm distance.
Contraindications: Fever, acute inflammation, hypertension, epilepsy, hyperthyroidism.

cards.title: Orange Light Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Wavelength: 590–620 nm.
Action: Stimulates lungs, pancreas and spleen; mild sympathetic activation; uplifts mood.
Indications: Asthma, bronchitis, kidney stones, constipation, hypothyroidism, menstrual irregularities, low energy states, mild depression.
Duration: 15–20 min.
Note: Gentler than red — suitable for patients who cannot tolerate full red light.
Contraindications: Fever, anxiety, hypertension.

cards.title: Yellow Light Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Wavelength: 570–590 nm.
Action: Stimulates motor neurons; tones lymphatic and digestive systems; mild laxative effect.
Indications: Constipation, indigestion, liver disorders, skin diseases (eczema, psoriasis), diabetes, nerve tonic, mild anxiety.
Duration: 20–30 min.
Contraindications: Acute fever, diarrhoea, inflammatory bowel conditions, hyperthyroidism.

cards.title: Green Light Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Wavelength: 495–570 nm.
Action: Balances autonomic nervous system; pituitary stimulant; antiseptic, bacteriostatic properties.
Indications: Hypertension, headache, neuralgias, ulcers, cancer supportive, heart conditions, eye strain, general debility.
Duration: 20–30 min.
Special Use: Green is the colour of balance — used when diagnosis is unclear or for general toning.
Contraindications: Hypotension.

cards.title: Blue Light Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Wavelength: 450–495 nm.
Action: Parasympathomimetic; sedates and cools; anti-inflammatory; reduces fever; inhibits bacterial growth.
Indications: Fever, inflammation, hypertension, insomnia, anxiety, hyperthyroidism, burns (minor), jaundice (neonatal), acne vulgaris.
Duration: 20–30 min. Can be used locally or systemically.
Contraindications: Depression, hypothyroidism, paralysis, cold conditions, anaemia.

cards.title: Indigo & Violet Light Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Indigo (420–450 nm): Parathyroid depressant; astringent; affects sinuses and eyes. Indications: cataract, sinusitis, nosebleeds, tonsillitis, deafness.
Violet (380–420 nm): Affects spleen and CNS; lymphatic stimulant. Indications: epilepsy, neuroses, scalp disorders, leucoderma, tumours (adjunct).
Duration: 10–15 min (both are potent — use shorter sessions).
Contraindications: Depression, hypothyroidism.

cards.title: Solarised Water
cards._legacyHtml:
Preparation: Fill coloured glass bottle (or wrap white glass bottle in coloured cellophane) with water. Expose to direct sunlight for 4–8 hours. Store away from light.
Dosage: 30–60 mL, 3× daily, 30 min before meals.
Colour Uses:
  • Blue water: Fever, inflammation, insomnia
  • Green water: Hypertension, ulcers
  • Yellow water: Constipation, liver disorders
  • Red water: Anaemia, weakness
Shelf life: Use within 24 hours. Do not refrigerate.

cards.title: LED Chromotherapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Devices: LED panels, fibre-optic beds, colour therapy lamps, handheld LED pens.
Advantages over glass filters: Precise wavelength, consistent output, no UV, no heat, safe for close proximity.
Common Protocols:
  • Red LED (660 nm): Wound healing, hair growth, arthritis
  • Near-infrared (850 nm): Deep tissue, musculoskeletal pain
  • Blue LED (415 nm): Acne, neonatal jaundice
Duration: 10–20 min per session. Course: 10–15 sessions.

cards.title: Chromotherapy — Contraindications & Safety
cards._legacyHtml:
General Contraindications:
  • Photosensitive conditions (lupus, porphyria, XP)
  • Photosensitising medications (tetracyclines, NSAIDs, diuretics)
  • Active skin cancer at treatment site
  • Directly over eyes (unless eye-specific protocol with protection)
  • Pregnancy (avoid red/infrared over abdomen)
Safety Rules: Always use UV-filtered lamps. Avoid staring into light source. Skin should be clean and dry. Check for hypersensitivity first session. Document colour, distance, duration, and response.
